manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: string, ball, lemon, tooth, lawyer, ray, spot, swing, comic.
Your opponent's team words are: england, shot, laser, atlantis, limousine, pass, pan, beijing.
Distractor words are: pin, snowman, cloak, war, triangle, chair, pitch.
The kill words are: heart.

message.content:
it.CLUE: tennis
TARGETS: ball, swing, string
message.reasoning:
We need give a single-word clue related to some of our team's words (string, ball, lemon... etc). Must not be any of the words in any list. Also can't contain parts of them. We choose some subset.

We need to be careful not to hint opponent or distractor. Provide CLUE and TARGETS.

Goal is to maximize number of correct guesses.

We can give clue that relates to multiple words: e.g., "cavity" relates to tooth, maybe lemon? Not.

Maybe "circus" relates to clown? Not.

We could do "fruit" for lemon only.

Better to relate to 2-3 words, like "swing" "ball" "string" all related to "golf". Golf uses ball, swing, string? Actually string not. "tennis": ball, swing, string (racquet strings). That could hint ball, swing, string. Also maybe "court". But "tennis" is a sports word not in lists. So clue "tennis" relates to ball, swing, string. Also "lawyer" maybe "court". That could be "court": relates to lawyer, ball (court ball - basketball?), swing (court? maybe tennis court), string (court? no). Might be too many ambiguous.

Better give two clues maybe focusing on subsets across turns. But they ask for one clue now. Provide whatever.

We must ensure not to hint opponent words. "tennis" not related to opponent words. Distractor words: pin, snowman, cloak, war, triangle, chair, pitch. "tennis" not those.

So we can give clue: "tennis". Targets: ball, swing, string. That seems safe.

Check: "string" associated with tennis racquet strings. "ball" obviously tennis ball. "swing" the swing of a racket. Works.

Make sure "tennis" not part of any list. Not.

Thus output:

CLUE: tennis
TARGETS: ball, swing, string

That's
